headline: Amazon, Microsoft cloud computing services should fall under EU tech rules, EU regulators say
summary: Amazon and Microsoft's cloud computing services should ‌be designated gatekeepers under EU ‌rules aimed at reining in the power ​of Big Tech, EU antitrust regulators said on Thursday. Amazon Web Services and Microsoft Azure, the two largest ‌cloud providers globally, ⁠should be designated gatekeepers under the Digital Markets Act ⁠which sets out a list of dos and don'ts to ensure ​a level ​playing field, ​the European Commission ‌said. The preliminary findings by the EU competition enforcer came after a seven-month long investigation.

content.title: Amazon, Microsoft cloud computing services should fall under EU tech rules, EU regulators say
content.summary: Amazon and Microsoft's cloud computing services should ‌be designated gatekeepers under EU ‌rules aimed at reining in the power ​of Big Tech, EU antitrust regulators said on Thursday. Amazon Web Services and Microsoft Azure, the two largest ‌cloud providers globally, ⁠should be designated gatekeepers under the Digital Markets Act ⁠which sets out a list of dos and don'ts to ensure ​a level ​playing field, ​the European Commission ‌said. The preliminary findings by the EU competition enforcer came after a seven-month long investigation.

headline: EU targets Amazon, Microsoft cloud units for Big Tech “gatekeeper” rules
summary: EU antitrust regulators said Amazon and Microsoft's cloud computing services should be designated as “gatekeepers” under landmark tech rules, ‌a step that would subject them to strict obligations aimed at curbing market ‌power.  The designation under the Digital Markets Act would impose a set of obligations and bans on the world's ​two largest cloud providers, Amazon Web Services and Microsoft Azure, including limits on self-preferencing and requirements to ensure interoperability and data portability.  So far, EU regulators have targeted core platform services such as search, social media and app stores to rein in Big Tech.

content.title: EU targets Amazon, Microsoft cloud units for Big Tech “gatekeeper” rules
content.summary: EU antitrust regulators said Amazon and Microsoft's cloud computing services should be designated as “gatekeepers” under landmark tech rules, ‌a step that would subject them to strict obligations aimed at curbing market ‌power.  The designation under the Digital Markets Act would impose a set of obligations and bans on the world's ​two largest cloud providers, Amazon Web Services and Microsoft Azure, including limits on self-preferencing and requirements to ensure interoperability and data portability.  So far, EU regulators have targeted core platform services such as search, social media and app stores to rein in Big Tech.

thumbnail.caption: Amazon logo at the company's logistics center near Paris

headline: Why Amazon Is Investing Another $13 Billion in India’s AI Data Centers
summary: Amazon  has committed an additional $13 billion to expand its artificial-intelligence and cloud infrastructure in India as it doubles down on the world’s most populous country.  This latest infusion brings  Amazon’s  total planned investment in India to $48 billion between 2026 and 2030.  The fresh capital will be used specifically to expand data center capacity in Mumbai and Hyderabad, Amazon said.

content.title: Why Amazon Is Investing Another $13 Billion in India’s AI Data Centers
content.summary: Amazon  has committed an additional $13 billion to expand its artificial-intelligence and cloud infrastructure in India as it doubles down on the world’s most populous country.  This latest infusion brings  Amazon’s  total planned investment in India to $48 billion between 2026 and 2030.  The fresh capital will be used specifically to expand data center capacity in Mumbai and Hyderabad, Amazon said.

headline: PitchBook Expands Premium AI Integrations with Microsoft 365 Copilot and Copilot in Excel
summary: SEATTLE, June 25, 2026--PitchBook, a leading private capital market intelligence platform, today announced a new federated Copilot connector with Microsoft, bringing trusted private capital market data into Microsoft 365 Copilot — including Copilot in Excel, Copilot Chat, and Researcher. The connector brings trusted private capital market data into secure enterprise AI environments and enables licensed users to interact with PitchBook intelligence directly within the Microsoft 365 Suite.

content.title: PitchBook Expands Premium AI Integrations with Microsoft 365 Copilot and Copilot in Excel

The connector brings trusted private capital market data into secure enterprise AI environments and enables licensed users to interact with PitchBook intelligence directly within the Microsoft 365 Suite.

headline: Xbox to raise console prices worldwide from August
summary: Microsoft's Xbox is raising prices of its gaming consoles by up to $150 worldwide, citing a ‌deepening global components crisis that has sent storage ‌and memory costs soaring across the consumer electronics industry.  Groups representing automakers, retailers, electronics ​firms and others had warned earlier this month that the increasing demand for memory chips could lead to dramatic price hikes in U.S. consumer goods and disrupt supply chains.  Effective August 1, ‌the price of Xbox ⁠consoles will increase by $100 for 512 GB models and $150 for 1 TB models.

content.title: Xbox to raise console prices worldwide from August
content.summary: Microsoft's Xbox is raising prices of its gaming consoles by up to $150 worldwide, citing a ‌deepening global components crisis that has sent storage ‌and memory costs soaring across the consumer electronics industry.  Groups representing automakers, retailers, electronics ​firms and others had warned earlier this month that the increasing demand for memory chips could lead to dramatic price hikes in U.S. consumer goods and disrupt supply chains.  Effective August 1, ‌the price of Xbox ⁠consoles will increase by $100 for 512 GB models and $150 for 1 TB models.

headline: Microsoft Stock Is Having Its Worst Start to a Year Since the Dot-Com Crash
summary: Heading into Thursday’s session, Microsoft stock had fallen more than 24% in 2026, putting it on track for its steepest first-half decline since a nearly 32% drop in 2000, according to Dow Jones Market Data.  For much of its history, Microsoft was best known as the pioneer behind the Windows operating system and wildly popular Office software.  Microsoft seemed like an obvious early winner in AI: The company became OpenAI’s first major corporate backer through a $1 billion investment in 2019, aligning itself early on with one of the industry’s juggernauts.

content.title: Microsoft Stock Is Having Its Worst Start to a Year Since the Dot-Com Crash

Microsoft seemed like an obvious early winner in AI: The company became OpenAI’s first major corporate backer through a $1 billion investment in 2019, aligning itself early on with one of the industry’s juggernauts.

headline: Microsoft Stock Is Having Its Worst Start to a Year Since the Dot-Com Crash
summary: Heading into Thursday’s session, Microsoft stock had fallen more than 24% in 2026, putting it on track for its steepest first-half decline since a nearly 32% drop in 2000, according to Dow Jones Market Data.  For much of its history, Microsoft was best known as the pioneer behind the Windows operating system and wildly popular Office software.  Microsoft seemed like an obvious early winner in AI: The company became OpenAI’s first major corporate backer through a $1 billion investment in 2019, aligning itself early on with one of the industry’s juggernauts.

content.title: Microsoft Stock Is Having Its Worst Start to a Year Since the Dot-Com Crash
content.summary: Heading into Thursday’s session, Microsoft stock had fallen more than 24% in 2026, putting it on track for its steepest first-half decline since a nearly 32% drop in 2000, according to Dow Jones Market Data.  For much of its history, Microsoft was best known as the pioneer behind the Windows operating system and wildly popular Office software.  Microsoft seemed like an obvious early winner in AI: The company became OpenAI’s first major corporate backer through a $1 billion investment in 2019, aligning itself early on with one of the industry’s juggernauts.

headline: Linux Foundation and Industry Leaders Launch Akrites to Defend Critical Open Source Software Against AI-Enabled Cyber Threats
summary: The Linux Foundation, the nonprofit organization enabling mass innovation through open source, today announced Akrites, a coordinated industry effort to harden the world's most critical open source software in the era of AI-assisted vulnerability discovery. Backed by founding commitments from Amazon Web Services, Anthropic, Chainguard, Cisco, Citi, Endor Labs, Ericsson, Google, IBM, JPMorganChase, Microsoft and GitHub, NVIDIA, OpenAI, RapidFort, Red Hat, Rust Foundation, Sonatype, Vodafone and Z

headline: Mag 7 Stocks Shed Nearly $3 Trillion This Month
summary: A massive rotation at the expense of big AI spenders has the Magnificent Seven on pace to shed $3 trillion this month.  Collectively, Tesla, Apple, Microsoft, Meta Platforms, Nvidia, Amazon.com, and Alphabet have lost $2.99 trillion in market cap so far in June, according to Dow Jones Market Data.  The Roundhill Magnificent Seven ETF is on pace for its worst month on record.

content.title: Mag 7 Stocks Shed Nearly $3 Trillion This Month
content.summary: A massive rotation at the expense of big AI spenders has the Magnificent Seven on pace to shed $3 trillion this month.  Collectively, Tesla, Apple, Microsoft, Meta Platforms, Nvidia, Amazon.com, and Alphabet have lost $2.99 trillion in market cap so far in June, according to Dow Jones Market Data.  The Roundhill Magnificent Seven ETF is on pace for its worst month on record.
